forecasts.reasoning: The recall for 303 pounds of ready-to-eat chicken Caesar wraps by LPK1 was issued on February 24, 2025. June 1, 2025 provides a horizon of approximately 97 days (about 3 months). While the amount recalled is exceptionally small and the product has a very short shelf life (meaning physical removal from commerce happens very quickly), the bureaucratic process for the USDA's FSIS to formally mark a recall 'Closed' can often lag significantly. FSIS effectiveness checks and the final administrative closure of the file frequently take longer than 3-4 months, leading cases to remain legally 'Active' on their website long after the public health risk has subsided. Given these characteristic administrative delays, a closure by June 1 seems slightly more likely than not to miss the deadline or be delayed on the website, though there's still a moderate chance the small scale triggers a faster bureaucratic closure.

President Trump signed an Executive Order on January 21, 2025, directing the renaming of the "Gulf of Mexico" to the "Gulf of America." By February 2025, the U.S. Geographic Names Information System (GNIS) and the Coast Guard had officially adopted the change, and Google Maps updated its display for U.S.-based users to read "Gulf of America." The background information of the prompt explicitly states that WorldAtlas.com has already updated its dedicated article for the body of water to "Gulf Of Mexico (Gulf of America)". This proves that the editorial team at WorldAtlas is aware of the change and is actively updating their platform to reflect this concurrent naming convention. The target page (the North America overview at na.htm) is highly visible. If the page uses an interactive API like Google Maps, the change has likely already automatically populated for U.S. users (matching the verification method by Metaculus admins). If it relies on text lists or custom graphics, WorldAtlas's demonstrated willingness to use the new name makes it highly likely they will update this page by July 1, 2025. The remaining 23% uncertainty accounts for the possibility that 'na.htm' is a legacy page utilizing a static map image that may be overlooked in site-wide updates before the deadline.

With less than two months until the resolution date (July 1, 2025), the probability of Beyond Meat filing for bankruptcy is extremely low. On May 7, 2025, the company announced its Q1 earnings, reporting a cash balance of $115.8 million against an operating cash burn of $26.1 million for the quarter. Crucially, they simultaneously announced a fresh $100 million secured financing facility from an affiliate of the Ahimsa Foundation. Coupled with the fact that their major $1.1 billion convertible note debt does not mature until March 2027 and the company confirming it has 'no near-term debt maturities', there is no liquidity crisis or debt deadline that would force a Chapter 11 filing in the next 54 days. Analysts and industry watchers largely cite 2027 as the base case for a potential bankruptcy filing if the balance sheet isn't restructured before then. I'm predicting 5%, the lowest allowed by the system constraints.

Egypt has demonstrated strong support for international sporting events with heavy government backing. Recently, Egypt's national traditional karate team won 1st place overall at the 2024 ITKF World Championship, establishing them as a premier nation in the sport. During the African Open Championship held in Cairo in late April 2025, Egyptian Minister of Youth and Sports Ashraf Sobhi met with the ITKF President to discuss hosting the 2026 World Championship. This active lobbying, combined with Egypt's strong capacity to host, makes them a very likely candidate to win the hosting rights. Small international federations like the ITKF often welcome government-backed bids and can process them relatively quickly. Considering the event is in 2026, the host needs to be announced soon (typically 1–1.5 years in advance). The main uncertainty is whether the official announcement happens before the tight deadline of July 1, 2025 (about two months after the discussion). However, the strong initial bid and the alignment of interests make a prompt announcement solid, so I estimate a 62% chance of the resolution conditions being met by July 1.

Sheikh Hasina is highly unlikely to return to Bangladesh between May 12 and July 1, 2025. Her son and political allies have indicated she intends to return only when the interim government announces elections, which are currently projected for late 2025 or early 2026. Voluntary return before then is exceedingly improbable, as she faces numerous criminal charges and the seizure of her assets by the interim administration, meaning she would immediately face arrest and potential mistreatment. 

Regarding involuntary return, while Bangladesh submitted a formal extradition request and Yunus raised the issue during a meeting with Indian PM Modi in April 2025, India has shown no inclination to comply. Given India's historical alliance with Hasina and the political nature of her charges, New Delhi has ample grounds under the 2013 bilateral extradition treaty to delay or reject the request. Even if India eventually agreed, the extradition process requires lengthy judicial scrutiny that would extend far beyond July 2025. Therefore, both voluntary return and extradition scenarios within the narrow resolution window are highly improbable.
